events.title: Power Slap 18: Luke Simonds vs. Peter Truchlik (Fight 1)
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Luke Simonds" if Luke Simonds is officially declared the winner of the fight against Peter Truchlik at Power Slap 18, scheduled for March 6, 2026.

It will resolve to "Peter Truchlik" if Peter Truchlik is officially declared the winner.

If the fight is declared a draw, ruled a No Contest, not scored, canceled, or postponed beyond March 20, 2026, this market will resolve "50-50."

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Power Slap.

eventMetadata.context_description: Undefeated middleweight knockout artist Stunt Marshall (2-0, both first-round KOs at Power Slap 13 and 15) faces former champion John Davis ("The Machine") in the Power Slap 19 opener on April 17 at The Cosmopolitan in Las Vegas, pitting raw power against veteran experience. Davis returns after an 18-month layoff since his title-losing KO to Isaih Quinones at Power Slap 9, leveraging his powerlifting background and early-career dominance including the inaugural belt win over Azael Rodriguez. Recent hype videos showcased their devastating strikes, with no confirmed injuries or withdrawals; coin toss determines first-striker advantage, crucial in slap fighting where flinching incurs fouls. Trader consensus weighs Marshall's momentum against Davis's proven durability in a stylistic clash of explosive offense versus tested chin.

eventMetadata.context_description: Austin Turpin enters Power Slap 19's heavyweight clash against TJ "The Gorilla" Thomas with a proven 7-2 record, highlighted by explosive knockouts like his 2025 Knockout of the Year and a recent unanimous decision win over Stevie Ray Ellis. Moving up from light heavyweight, Turpin weighed in at 265 lbs, showcasing superior size and power against Thomas's 222 lbs on short-notice entry following Thomas's mixed results, including a Power Slap 6 DQ victory and Power Slap 10 bout. No injuries or withdrawals reported post-weigh-ins and coin tosses; stylistic matchup favors Turpin's devastating right-hand striking and veteran recovery over Thomas's raw heavyweight power in this three-round Fight 8 undercard bout at The Cosmopolitan in Las Vegas.

eventMetadata.context_description: Destiny "My Time" McCubbin enters Power Slap 19 undefeated at 2-0 with wins via KO and decision, putting her perfect record on the line against Hollyhood Haley J (1-1) in the women's flyweight bout, as McCubbin won the coin toss to strike first. J boasts a pro wrestling pedigree, a debut DQ victory at Power Slap 13, and a recent TKO loss to Ellie Dempster at Power Slap 15, bringing size advantages including a 4-inch height edge and 3-inch reach lead. No injury concerns or weigh-in issues reported ahead of the April 17 clash at The Cosmopolitan in Las Vegas during WrestleMania weekend; their hyped friendship-rivalry fuels trader interest in early KO potential versus durability to unanimous decision.

eventMetadata.context_description: Defending Power Slap heavyweight champion Damien "The Bell" Dibbell (6-0), boasting four successful title defenses highlighted by his Round 2 KO of Wesley Mena at Power Slap 14, holds the edge over #3 contender Darius "The Destroyer" Mata-Varona (3-3) in this 5-round title clash. Dibbell's precision power and durability contrast Mata-Varona's explosive slaps and viral heavyweight background, with the champ winning the coin toss to strike first for a tactical advantage. Both cleared official weigh-ins on April 15 at 255lbs and 265lbs respectively, reporting no injuries or camp disruptions; recent hype underscores Dibbell's striking momentum driving trader consensus on his experience in close-range, knockout-or-survive exchanges.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jake Hager delivered a statement Power Slap debut at Power Slap 19 on April 17, 2026, stopping Devin "Big Jinxx" Jenkins via third-round knockout on the heavyweight main card from The Chelsea at The Cosmopolitan in Las Vegas. The former WWE World Heavyweight Champion and 3-0 MMA veteran absorbed Jenkins' opening clubbing slap without buckling, then landed devastating strikes that left the 0-2 Power Slapper unable to rise, confirming pre-fight trader consensus on Hager's superior power, chin durability, and combat sports pedigree from wrestling and Bellator. No weigh-in misses, injuries, or camp disruptions altered expectations, with Hager's six-fight deal signaling long-term commitment amid Dana White's promotion during WrestleMania week.

eventMetadata.context_description: Defending Power Slap Super Heavyweight Champion Dayne "Da Hawaiian Hitman" Viernes collides with unbeaten #1 contender Makini "Big Mak" Manu in Fight 7, the main event title bout at Power Slap 19 held April 17 in Las Vegas. Viernes, with a 4-2 record featuring TKOs like his R2 stoppage of Denis Vildanov and a majority decision title win over Vasily Kamotsky at Power Slap 13, made weight at 385 lbs during official weigh-ins. Manu entered 5-0 with devastating knockouts, including finishes against Duane Crespo and Dumpling, showcasing superior power in recent super heavyweight clashes. Absent injury reports or withdrawals, trader consensus reflects the champion's proven durability and experience versus the challenger's knockout momentum and stylistic edge in raw hand speed.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vasilii "Dumpling" Kamotskii, the #3-ranked super heavyweight with a durable 4-1-2 Power Slap record and never knocked out in the promotion, entered Power Slap 19 as the consensus favorite against #8 Eviahn "The Waterboy" Scott (1-3-0), who weighed in heavier at 382.5 lbs to Kamotskii's 348 lbs. Recent weigh-ins and coin toss—won by Scott for first strike—set the stage for their 3-round super heavyweight clash at The Chelsea in Las Vegas on April 17. Kamotskii aimed to rebound from recent decision setbacks with his proven chin and experience, while Scott sought an upset leveraging size advantage in a matchup highlighting raw power and endurance. Trader consensus reflected Kamotskii's stylistic edge in high-stakes slap bouts.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the heavyweight division at Power Slap 19, #6-ranked Pono “Da Hawaiian Assassin” Pau clashes with #8 Johnny “The New Mexican Badass” Magna in a scheduled 3-round bout from The Chelsea at The Cosmopolitan in Las Vegas. Pau enters on a hot streak after his knockout of James Siekmann at Power Slap 13, showcasing veteran power and resilience from prior decisions like his split win over Dorian Perez, while expressing intent to prove himself against Magna. The pro wrestler Magna remains undefeated, boasting crushing knockouts such as his Power Slap 15 early prelim finish and top PowerKube scores, highlighting raw striking force. With mutual respect, no reported injuries post-weigh-ins, and coin toss winners set, traders assess Pau's momentum versus Magna's knockout upside in this stylistic power matchup.
